description: Miami residents experience an average of 3.8 power outages per year, with each outage lasting approximately 8.2 hours. Located in one of the most hurricane-prone regions in the country, Miami homeowners served by Florida Power & Light face significant risks from hurricanes, flooding, and extreme heat. A whole-house generator ensures your AC, refrigerator, and essential systems stay powered during the next storm.

Tampa sits at the top of Tampa Bay, making it extremely vulnerable to storm surge during hurricanes. Tampa Electric customers experience an average of 3.5 outages per year. With lightning capital status and increasing hurricane activity, Tampa homeowners are investing in standby generators at record rates to protect their families and homes.

Houston homeowners face a unique combination of threats: Gulf Coast hurricanes, flash flooding, extreme summer heat, and the growing risk of winter storms like the devastating 2021 freeze. CenterPoint Energy customers experience an average of 3.2 outages per year, with some lasting over 9 hours. A standby generator is essential for maintaining comfort and safety in Houston's unpredictable climate.

New Orleans experiences some of the longest and most frequent power outages in the nation — an average of 5 outages per year lasting 14 hours each. From Hurricane Katrina to Hurricane Ida, the city's aging infrastructure and low-lying geography make reliable backup power a necessity, not a luxury. Entergy New Orleans customers consistently rank among the most outage-affected in the country.

Charleston's historic Lowcountry location makes it highly vulnerable to hurricanes, storm surge, and flooding. Dominion Energy customers experience an average of 4.2 outages per year. Hurricane Helene in 2024 reminded Charleston residents that backup power isn't optional — it's the difference between staying safe at home and being displaced for days.

Charlotte sits in the Piedmont region where ice storms and severe thunderstorms are the primary causes of power outages. Duke Energy customers experience an average of 2.5 outages per year. As Charlotte's population booms, the aging power grid faces increasing strain — making a home generator a smart investment for protecting your family and home office.

Atlanta's dense tree canopy is beautiful but dangerous during storms — falling limbs cause widespread power outages every year. Georgia Power customers experience an average of 2.8 outages per year. Between tornado season, severe thunderstorms, and occasional ice events, Atlanta homeowners are increasingly turning to standby generators for reliable backup power.

Los Angeles homeowners face Public Safety Power Shutoffs (PSPS) during wildfire season, when utilities cut power to prevent fires. LADWP and SoCal Edison customers can lose power for days during these events. Generator installations in LA are more expensive due to seismic bracing requirements, strict permitting, and higher labor costs — but the investment pays for itself during the first extended shutoff.

Detroit winters are harsh, and DTE Energy customers are no strangers to multi-day power outages during ice storms and blizzards. With an average of 3 outages per year lasting 8 hours each, losing heat in sub-zero temperatures isn't just uncomfortable — it can be dangerous. A natural gas standby generator keeps your furnace, sump pump, and lights running when you need them most.

Nashville sits in Dixie Alley, one of the most tornado-prone corridors in the country. The devastating 2020 tornado outbreak was a wake-up call for many Nashville homeowners. Nashville Electric Service customers experience an average of 3.2 outages per year. Between tornado season and winter ice storms, a standby generator provides critical protection for Nashville families.
